name: GOLFTEC Orland Park
about: GOLFTEC Orland Park is a golf training and instruction center that offers a high-tech, data-driven approach to golf improvement. The facility specializes in personalized golf lessons, custom club fittings, and technology-enhanced practice sessions. Utilizing advanced equipment like OPTIMOTION motion tracking, high-speed cameras, and launch monitors, their certified PGA coaches provide precise feedback to help golfers of all skill levels refine their swings and improve on-course performance. The center is designed for players focused on serious skill development rather than casual entertainment, emphasizing measurable results and long-term game strategy.
why_golfers_like_it: Data-driven instruction using OPTIMOTION technology and launch monitors for precise swing analysis
Certified PGA coaches provide personalized improvement plans tailored to individual goals
Custom club fitting service adds an average of 21 yards off the tee using advanced fitting technology

simulator_hardware.notes: Advanced launch monitor technology used for lessons and fittings
Mentioned for practice bay technology and simulator experiences

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, players should bring their own golf clubs for lessons and practice sessions. The facility also offers club rentals and provides all necessary high-tech equipment for analysis.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food or drinks?
faq.answer: Outside food and drinks are not mentioned, suggesting they may not be permitted. The facility focuses on training without apparent food service options.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: Virtual golf simulator sessions at GOLFTEC vary based on player preference and goals rather than set rounds, as the focus is on skill development rather than traditional play.

faq.question: Is this venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, GOLFTEC offers instruction programs specifically designed for juniors and youth golfers, with technology-based teaching methods that can appeal to younger players.
